content:
# Final Review Arbiter
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Accepted the disputed amendment because the early threshold return suppresses the documented existing-bead mapping step, even though that step does not create a new bead.

## Rulings

- `fr-1-8eeee862`: Accept
  The amendment identifies a real ordering defect. In src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, current_count < threshold records Pending with existing_bead_lookup_ran=false and returns at lines 5189-5209, before the policy.enabled existing-bead lookup at lines 5213-5244. The controlling bead policy says propose-new-bead handling must first check whether an existing bead owns the work and only then create a new bead; the threshold is a guard on creation, not on no-new-bead de-duplication/commenting. So a below-threshold but strong existing match can be left as Pending instead of CoveredByExistingBead.
